durableFields.domainInvariant: At most one generic protection/charm/ward route subject is offered at a time; resolved creature-protection and sanctuary witnesses are derived at adapter boundaries from fill facts and canonical battle fields.

Setup can admit one generic target-admission, active-effect, condition-lifecycle, saving-throw-roll-mode, or target-interdiction subject without carrying resolved projection state or authored spell identity dispatch.
Action availability is the battle-state source of whether the actor can still spend an action after resolving diagnostic protection/charm/ward rows.
First-level slot expenditure is the battle-state source of the spell resource consumed by diagnostic protection/charm rows.
Fighter hit points are the battle-state source observed by ward teardown rows that deal damage.

nonDurableBoundaryApiSurface.boundary: adapter-facing transient witness derived from branch fill and canonical BattleState action, spell-slot, and hit-point fields
production reducer route observer trace for accepted FU01D protection/charm/ward route replay derived from copied route connectors
non-durable target replay artifact recording accepted FU01D route-event projections against copied route connectors
checked target replay field proving FU01D branches route through generic reducer subject kinds rather than authored identity
checked target replay field proving FU01D qRoute projections use copied generic subject families
